'''Reytha''' was one of the most productive [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworlds]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and later the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. At the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Reytha was governed by [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Antes Belladar]].

==Geography and Ecology==
[[File:Muja_Fruit.jpg|thumb|left|Muja bushes on Reytha]]
Reytha's microclimates ensured that it had suitable growing conditions all year round. At the peak of the Empire's power, genetic modifications like special synthesizers and genetic inducer-droids were introduced on a greater scale, and the planet became known as the 'breadbasket of the Empire,'<ref name="TCSWE" /> producing nearly 10% of the food for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] military.<ref name="SW:GB" /> Its major crop was [[Muja fruit/Legends|muja fruit]]; [[Reythan berry|Reythan berries]] were also produced there. Reytha's seas and oceans were teeming with fish and were almost as productive as its agricultural fields.

==History==
Reytha was an [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]] which belonged to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="TCSWE" />

Reytha's main settlement was the city of [[Yeere]], which held the only [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] on the planet and was also [[Garrison/Legends|garrisoned]] with a meager number of troops. During major conflicts, when agriworlds became prime targets, Yeere had been the location of reinforced [[Fortress/Legends|fortresses]] and strongholds.

The large [[Montrosa Ocean]] kept the planet cool and drove the bountiful rains that nourished the soil. The ocean was the location of an island chain popular with [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] star tours.<ref name="SW:GB DataBank">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' DataBank, Reytha entry</ref>

Shortly after the Battle of Yavin, when [[Antes Belladar]] was the [[Governor/Legends|Governor]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] captured Reytha, depriving the Empire of valuable food resources. The Empire's [[Executor/Legends|Executor]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], learned of the strike from a mind probe of Rebel [[General/Legends|General]] [[Jan Dodonna/Legends|Jan Dodonna]], was sent to take it back. As [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] looked on in secret, Vader eventually [[Reytha campaign|recaptured]] Reytha, despite Governor Belladar's many delaying tactics. Convinced Beladar was either a Rebel sympathizer, or was too incompetent to evict all Rebel presence on the planet, Vader executed him.<ref name="SW:GB" />

By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the planet had become an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]].<ref name="TEA" /> Reytha was [[Battle of Reytha|conquered]] by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[New Republic Third Fleet|Third Fleet]] under command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Willham Burke]] in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]].<ref name="TEGtW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Reytha was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] for the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. It serves as the location of two consecutive Darth Vader missions, which were also included on the game's [[2002]] expansion pack, ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''. The planet was also mentioned in the [[2004]] novel ''[[Yoda: Dark Rendezvous]]''.
